clean_text: Assalamu alaikum thank you for joining us on our latest installment of zadil authored by the late an esteemed scholar of islam we were we had reached a point where we were discussing the way that the prophet muhammad sallallahu alaihi wasallam would recite the quran and as a prelude to this i'd to mention something that is very important it's a statement of the prophet sallallahu alaihi wasallam it's collected by both bukhari and muslim that he said mayuri whomever allah desires good for whoever is essentially destined for good and ultimately destined for paradise allah will grant him understanding of the religion now what does that mean does that mean that only scholars are people whom allah has desired good for no that's not what is intended what is intended is that a person not become acquainted with every aspect of the religion of islam though that is the ultimate of blessing from allah subhanahu wa'ta'ala rather what's intended by this narration as the scholars of islam have mentioned is that if allah desires good for a person they will become acquainted with all aspects that concern them all aspects of his or her religion of his or her worship of allah which is particular to their set of circumstances right so if a person is a merchant somebody who buys and sells then if allah desires good for this individual he will open the gates of knowledge upon this person to become acquainted with the do's and don'ts as regards to the religion of allah in that particular field and so the reason for this is that the worship of allah subhanahu wa ta'ala is something that allah has given permission i know that that's kind of an interesting concept it is the responsibility of everyone to worship allah yes but we have to understand that one of the great honors that allah has given the human being is permission to worship him subhanahu wa that he has been permitted to consciously exercise his or her free will to worship the the lord of the heavens and the earth allah subhanahu wa ta'ala has said about the masajid about the mosques he says oh he describes the masjid as houses which allah has granted permission that they be erected and that his name be mentioned therein and so the scholars of islam mention that when we worship allah when we worship allah subhanahu wa'ta'ala we have actually been given permission to do so now what are the implications of that the implications of that are that an individual may not worship allah without having permission without having guidance on every single thing that you do this is more commonly known as worship and customs they say the nature of worship is that it is impermissible unless otherwise stated now with regards to our customs it is exactly the opposite what do i mean by customs what we eat what we drink how we sit how we sleep when we sleep what we do these things that are customary across all lines muslim and non-muslim alike right this is our customs as a human being this is how you are the natural state of our customs is that everything is permissible except what allah has prohibited it's the opposite of worship so anytime a person worships we say wait stop right there do you have explicit evidence for this warship if the answer is yes then that is what allah has given you permission if the answer is no then a person may not do it until he or she is positive that this is something that allah has allowed this is where the concept of innovation comes from that if a person worships allah in a way that allah subhanahu wa ta'ala has not granted permission this is what innovation is and as the prophet sallallahu alaihi wasallam informed us all innovation is rejected right out the gates now that is contrary to our customs with the customs it's exactly the opposite allah doesn't tell us what to do right he doesn't tell us what to eat he doesn't tell us what to wear he doesn't tell us what to drink rather he tells us what not to drink he tells us what not to wear he tells us what not to eat right and so this is taken from the story of adam alaihissalam the first story in the quran allah tells adam with regards to customs with regards to our nature with regards to our customs he says subhanahu to adam live you and your spouse herein in this paradise and eat from anywhere you choose but do not approach or come near this one tree right it could be one particular tree or a type of tree so allah subhanahu wa ta'ala doesn't tell him what to eat he tells him what not to eat now let's look at worship when adam alaihissalam did what was prohibited he disobeyed and he wished to seek forgiveness he wished to repent well repentance is a major worship of allah and so adam alaihissalam was instructed what to do in that case and so allah subhanahu wa'ta'ala says with regards to adam's repentance that adam received words and instruction from his lord and meaning he said those words as he was instructed and allah accepted his repentance so we see now a contrast with regards to what to eat what to wear our customs do whatever you like except for these things which are prohibited yet when it comes to the worship of allah you may not do anything without explicit instruction and permission from allah and so often times people don't know how to learn if we understand that learning how to worship allah is so crucial how do i go about acquiring knowledge well this is an a topic which actually many books from times of ancient from as far back as the second century after hijola the scholars of islam have written books on the methodology and the etiquettes of seeking knowledge and one of the questions that i get a lot all of the time a common theme and that is the question goes something this or let me say let me give you a scenario and tell me if this sounds familiar you get this sudden urge to learn you become absolutely enthralled with islamic knowledge whether it be the quran the sunnah reading narrations whatever it is you become almost obsessed with it and you read everything you can get your hands on and this goes on for about one or two or three days or maybe one or two or three weeks and then all of the sudden just as quickly as it came you feel if you read another thing you're going to you you just have no capacity for reading anything else you're just done and then we have this process continue you either are sprinting or you're standing still there's nothing in between why does that happen well there's a number of reasons but one of the main reasons is that you are trying to do too much all at once you are trying to do too much all at once you have to remember that your threshold for knowledge and reading and research and it's it's a meditation it's something that is a muscle it has to be built and you cannot go to the gym and pick up power you know all the weights in the world at once and think that that's the shortcut there are no shortcuts in knowledge the prophet salallahu only comes by seeking knowledge there's only one path to it there's no way to short circuit that path or to take a shortcut or cut corners that is not how knowledge is sought it is something that is a process if you try to deviate from that process you do so at your own peril and so the process is if you want this knowledge then you have to seek it and seeking knowledge is something which a person develops over a period of time it's a muscle it's your memory actually which is also very similar to a muscle and what's interesting about it is once you develop that threshold of sitting and studying and learning if you can sit for one hour and then two hours and then three and then you know all the way up to however many hours you practice this is something that you have trained yourself to do and if you stop doing it you don't magically have it forever no if you stop doing it your muscle your threshold for study for learning starts to regress that's how it works same thing with your memory if you train your memory constantly you will become very very good at memorizing things quickly and if you stop practicing you lose your sharpness and so if let's say if your threshold for studying or reading something is a half an hour right then i would say always stop while you are still interested never stop once you've reached your full threshold so if your threshold is 30 minutes after 30 minutes you just can't look at a book anymore then read for 20 minutes always keep that flame don't ever exhaust it always keep it there so that you can continue to return and so knowledge is sought by number one consistency this is always important 10 minutes a day is better than one hour a week 10 minutes a day is better than three hours a month this is not how we learn we do not learn by studying all at once and then leaving everything all at once we learn by being consistent second of all intensity is what we are looking for not extensity we don't really care how long you study what we care is how intense that study is how collected your mind is when you read when you are learning third of all you need to focus your energy on one thing you can't just have 20 different books and read a little bit from every single book and become a mile wide and an inch deep this is not going to be of consequence to anyone knowledge that will fortify you in these times of uncertainty and these times of fitnah and these times of misguidance where the prophet sallallahu alaihi wasallam said hastened to do good deeds before trials and tribulations will come that are like parts of a black faith comes with unshaken understanding this is why allah explains to us the deeper your knowledge goes the deeper you are planting that seed in your heart and so what is very important is that a person always go for depth don't move on to something else until you've learned that don't turn the page until you've understood that page completely if you read one page every day great as long as it is an intense understanding the scholars of islam have told us they give an example of seeking knowledge an individual who is hunting a hunter if you could imagine with me a hunter goes into a forest he thinks oh actually that's better so he then takes aim at the turkey and then he sees a small deer so then he thinks no that's even better and just as he's going to the small deer he sees a buck and just as he's go that hunter if he continues on he is going to go home hungry he will not have gone home with the buck nor will he have gone home with the squirrel why because one of the worst things paragraphs: Assalamu alaikum thank you for joining us on our latest installment of zadil authored by the late an esteemed scholar of islam we were we had reached a point where we were discussing the way that the prophet muhammad sallallahu alaihi wasallam would recite the quran and as a prelude to this i'd to mention something that is very important it's a statement of the prophet sallallahu alaihi wasallam it's collected by both bukhari and muslim that he said mayuri whomever allah desires good for whoever is essentially destined for good and ultimately destined for paradise allah will grant him understanding of the religion now what does that mean does that mean that only scholars are people whom allah has desired good for no that's not what is intended what is intended is that a person not become acquainted with every aspect of the religion of islam though that is the ultimate of blessing from allah subhanahu wa'ta'ala rather what's intended by this narration as the scholars of islam have mentioned is that if allah desires good for a person they will become acquainted with all aspects that concern them all aspects of his or her religion of his or her worship of allah which is particular to their set of circumstances right so if a person is a merchant somebody who buys and sells then if allah desires good for this individual he will open the gates of knowledge upon this person to become acquainted with the do's and

don'ts as regards to the religion of allah in that particular field and so the reason for this is that the worship of allah subhanahu wa ta'ala is something that allah has given permission i know that that's kind of an interesting concept it is the responsibility of everyone to worship allah yes but we have to understand that one of the great honors that allah has given the human being is permission to worship him subhanahu wa that he has been permitted to consciously exercise his or her free will to worship the the lord of the heavens and the earth allah subhanahu wa ta'ala has said about the masajid about the mosques he says oh he describes the masjid as houses which allah has granted permission that they be erected and that his name be mentioned therein and so the scholars of islam mention that when we worship allah when we worship allah subhanahu wa'ta'ala we have actually been given permission to do so now what are the implications of that the implications of that are that an individual may not worship allah without having permission without having guidance on every single thing that you do this is more commonly known as worship and customs they say the nature of worship is that it is impermissible unless otherwise stated now with regards to our customs it is exactly the opposite what do i mean by customs what we eat what we drink how we sit how we sleep

when we sleep what we do these things that are customary across all lines muslim and non-muslim alike right this is our customs as a human being this is how you are the natural state of our customs is that everything is permissible except what allah has prohibited it's the opposite of worship so anytime a person worships we say wait stop right there do you have explicit evidence for this warship if the answer is yes then that is what allah has given you permission if the answer is no then a person may not do it until he or she is positive that this is something that allah has allowed this is where the concept of innovation comes from that if a person worships allah in a way that allah subhanahu wa ta'ala has not granted permission this is what innovation is and as the prophet sallallahu alaihi wasallam informed us all innovation is rejected right out the gates now that is contrary to our customs with the customs it's exactly the opposite allah doesn't tell us what to do right he doesn't tell us what to eat he doesn't tell us what to wear he doesn't tell us what to drink rather he tells us what not to drink he tells us what not to wear he tells us what not to eat right and so this is taken from the story of adam alaihissalam the first story in the quran allah tells adam with regards to

customs with regards to our nature with regards to our customs he says subhanahu to adam live you and your spouse herein in this paradise and eat from anywhere you choose but do not approach or come near this one tree right it could be one particular tree or a type of tree so allah subhanahu wa ta'ala doesn't tell him what to eat he tells him what not to eat now let's look at worship when adam alaihissalam did what was prohibited he disobeyed and he wished to seek forgiveness he wished to repent well repentance is a major worship of allah and so adam alaihissalam was instructed what to do in that case and so allah subhanahu wa'ta'ala says with regards to adam's repentance that adam received words and instruction from his lord and meaning he said those words as he was instructed and allah accepted his repentance so we see now a contrast with regards to what to eat what to wear our customs do whatever you like except for these things which are prohibited yet when it comes to the worship of allah you may not do anything without explicit instruction and permission from allah and so often times people don't know how to learn if we understand that learning how to worship allah is so crucial how do i go about acquiring knowledge well this is an a topic which actually many books from times of ancient from as far back as the second century

after hijola the scholars of islam have written books on the methodology and the etiquettes of seeking knowledge and one of the questions that i get a lot all of the time a common theme and that is the question goes something this or let me say let me give you a scenario and tell me if this sounds familiar you get this sudden urge to learn you become absolutely enthralled with islamic knowledge whether it be the quran the sunnah reading narrations whatever it is you become almost obsessed with it and you read everything you can get your hands on and this goes on for about one or two or three days or maybe one or two or three weeks and then all of the sudden just as quickly as it came you feel if you read another thing you're going to you you just have no capacity for reading anything else you're just done and then we have this process continue you either are sprinting or you're standing still there's nothing in between why does that happen well there's a number of reasons but one of the main reasons is that you are trying to do too much all at once you are trying to do too much all at once you have to remember that your threshold for knowledge and reading and research and it's it's a meditation it's something that is a muscle it has to be built and you cannot go to the gym

and pick up power you know all the weights in the world at once and think that that's the shortcut there are no shortcuts in knowledge the prophet salallahu only comes by seeking knowledge there's only one path to it there's no way to short circuit that path or to take a shortcut or cut corners that is not how knowledge is sought it is something that is a process if you try to deviate from that process you do so at your own peril and so the process is if you want this knowledge then you have to seek it and seeking knowledge is something which a person develops over a period of time it's a muscle it's your memory actually which is also very similar to a muscle and what's interesting about it is once you develop that threshold of sitting and studying and learning if you can sit for one hour and then two hours and then three and then you know all the way up to however many hours you practice this is something that you have trained yourself to do and if you stop doing it you don't magically have it forever no if you stop doing it your muscle your threshold for study for learning starts to regress that's how it works same thing with your memory if you train your memory constantly you will become very very good at memorizing things quickly and if you stop practicing you lose your sharpness and so

if let's say if your threshold for studying or reading something is a half an hour right then i would say always stop while you are still interested never stop once you've reached your full threshold so if your threshold is 30 minutes after 30 minutes you just can't look at a book anymore then read for 20 minutes always keep that flame don't ever exhaust it always keep it there so that you can continue to return and so knowledge is sought by number one consistency this is always important 10 minutes a day is better than one hour a week 10 minutes a day is better than three hours a month this is not how we learn we do not learn by studying all at once and then leaving everything all at once we learn by being consistent second of all intensity is what we are looking for not extensity we don't really care how long you study what we care is how intense that study is how collected your mind is when you read when you are learning third of all you need to focus your energy on one thing you can't just have 20 different books and read a little bit from every single book and become a mile wide and an inch deep this is not going to be of consequence to anyone knowledge that will fortify you in these times of uncertainty and these times of fitnah and these times of misguidance where the

prophet sallallahu alaihi wasallam said hastened to do good deeds before trials and tribulations will come that are like parts of a black and dark night a person would be a believer in the morning and become a disbeliever by night that quickly the reason for this is that we are not focusing and having any depth to our knowledge unshaken faith comes with unshaken understanding this is why allah explains to us the deeper your knowledge goes the deeper you are planting that seed in your heart and so what is very important is that a person always go for depth don't move on to something else until you've learned that don't turn the page until you've understood that page completely if you read one page every day great as long as it is an intense understanding the scholars of islam have told us they give an example of seeking knowledge an individual who is hunting a hunter if you could imagine with me a hunter goes into a forest and sees a sees a squirrel so he levels his rifle at the squirrel and then just as he's going to he has made his decision that he's going to have squirrel for dinner and then he sees a turkey so then he thinks oh actually that's better so he then takes aim at the turkey and then he sees a small deer so then he thinks no that's even better and just as he's going to the small

deer he sees a buck and just as he's go that hunter if he continues on he is going to go home hungry he will not have gone home with the buck nor will he have gone home with the squirrel why because one of the worst things that the human being can have in his path to knowledge that will impede his progress is options too many options and right now we live in a time where information is coming at us with such speed you can go to a libra you can go to a bookstore and walk out with thousands of volumes all at once this is not good for anyone because what ends up happening is you have too many options you end up never committing to any one of those options and so this is how knowledge is sought this is knowledge people may you know people may read this and say oh it's so so dry or it's so unengaging no it's very engaging it's just that this is what knowledge is there is a place and there is a time for motivational speeches there's a time for inspirational quotes and inspirational stories and there's also a time for actually having clear instruction and this is what these classes are designed for and so it's something that takes patience something that takes determination person must always remind himself why it is he is doing this now continuing on with the recitation of the prophet

summary: This is part 9 of a series on Zadul-Ma'ad (Provisions for the Hereafter) by the late Islamic scholar, led by Sheikh Ali Mashhour. The lecture begins with a foundational hadith from both Sahih Bukhari and Sahih Muslim about Allah granting understanding of the religion to those destined for good, explaining that this applies to all believers acquiring knowledge relevant to their circumstances. Sheikh Mashhour then discusses the crucial distinction between worship ('ibadah) and customs ('adat): worship requires explicit Islamic evidence and permission, while customs are permissible unless prohibited. He emphasizes that innovation in worship is strictly forbidden. The second major section addresses the proper methodology for seeking Islamic knowledge, stressing consistency over intensity, depth over breadth, and the importance of developing one's learning capacity gradually like building a muscle. He warns against the common pattern of intense study bursts followed by complete cessation, and advocates for daily, focused study of a single subject. Finally, the lecture transitions to discussing the Prophet Muhammad's method of Qur'anic recitation, specifically his elongated and measured recitation style, and the proper way to say 'Amen' during congregational prayer—timing it with the imam's recitation and maintaining unified pronunciation with the congregation.
suggested_title: Understanding Worship vs. Customs and the Proper Methodology for Seeking Islamic Knowledge
catchy_title: Building Your Knowledge Like a Muscle
